Research. HCI + AI
Making Claude the best knowledge coworker with taste

experience.title: Staff Researcher & Manager
experience.description:
Apple Intelligence
My team built ML infra to automate compression of Apple Intelligence models. Our models run fast (30 token/sec on iPhone) on‐device and on private cloud compute, while achieving similar accuracy after 10x compression. In addition to auto-optimization pipeline, Talaria (built by my team) guides the fine‐grained compression rate selection.

Vision Pro
Talaria also supported latency optimization of Vision Pro ML models and iPhone camera models. It can simulate on‐device performance (e.g., latency, power) within seconds, and rapidly estimate performance improvement from layer‐level optimization. This significantly reduced time spent on model benchmark and optimization decisions. https://machinelearning.apple.com/research/talaria (CHI best paper nominee)

ML-powered Accessibility
My early projects at Apple improved mobile app accessibility with applied machine learning:
• Understand app UI to improve VoiceOver
https://machinelearning.apple.com/research/creating-accessibility-metadata (CHI best paper)
https://machinelearning.apple.com/research/recommending-accessibility (W4A best paper)
https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT211899
• On-the-fly calibration for improved on-device eye tracking
https://patents.google.com/patent/US11106280B1/en

experience.title: Research Intern
experience.description: Microsoft Research Enable Team. Built a low-cost, robust, portable, easy-to-learn and fast gaze gesture communication phone app for people with ALS.

projects.name: AResume
projects.description:
●	Segmented and enhanced the photo of resume, then recognized its keywords.
●	Let recruiters see an Augmented layer through iPad containing online information extracted from resume keywords:  Portfolio videos / Facebook connection in recruiting company / LinkedIn endorsed skills / Technical keywords in Github.
●	Got second Place in Facebook SoCal Hackathon out of 200+ competitors.

projects.name: EyeWeight
projects.description:
● Provided visualized negative stimulus (an AR layer) on unhealthy food.
● Kept track of eating history and made recommendations.
● Encourages diet competition with strangers.
● Won LA Hacks Best overall, Best mobile app and Best data visualization.
